title: ANTI JOIN & WHERE NOT EXISTS produce different results when handles >, >=, <, <=
description:
### What happens?

`ANTI JOIN` and `WHERE NOT EXISTS` are semantically equivalent. However,  they produce different results when handles >, >=, <, <=.

### To Reproduce

```sql
-- WHEN c0 and c1 are VARCHAR, these bugs can not be triggered.
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c1 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);

-- These equivalent anti-join queries (i.e., ANTI JOIN = WHERE NOT EXISTS) return the same results when using ComparisonOperator: =, !=, BETWEEN, NOT BETWEEN, INпјҢ NOT IN, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL
-- positive cases
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 = t1.c1);
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 = t1.c1;

SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 != t1.c1);
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 != t1.c1;

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ   1   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

-- These equivalent anti-join queries (i.e., ANTI JOIN = WHERE NOT EXISTS) return different results when using ComparisonOperator: >, >=, <, <=
-- negative cases
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 > t1.c1;
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 >= t1.c1;
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 < t1.c1;
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.c0 <= t1.c1;
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32  в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 0 rows в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 > t1.c1);
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 >= t1.c1);
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 < t1.c1);
SELECT t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 <= t1.c1);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ   1   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
